_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: > 3 days of watery stool per day
Bloody stool in case of Dysentery

ppd_preauth.q: Was the degree of Dehydration mentioned in clinical notes?
Was the duration of diarrhoea mentioned?

common_queries: Duration of diarrhoea not mentioned for Dysentery during pre-authorization
Evidence of blood in stool not found in clinical notes or history for Dysentery
Clinical notes with indications missing at pre-authorization stage
Planned line of management missing at pre-authorization stage
Discharge summary not available at claim stage
Indoor case papers missing at claim stage

clinical_key_pointers: > 3 days of watery stool per day
Acute Diarrhoea < 14 days, Chronic Diarrhoea >28 days
Bloody stool in case of Dysentery
additional_information.referral_criteria: Severe malnutrition/HIV; Severe dehydration; Hypernatremic (Na >145 mmol/L)/hyponatremic dehydration (Na <135 mmol/L); Dysentery with age <1 yr/ measles in past 6 weeks/dehydration/sick; Dysentery with no improvement on antibiotics; Persistent diarrhea with dehydration; Persistent diarrhea with serious systemic infection such as pneumonia, sepsis, infants <4 months of age, or when there is no improvement with treatment over 5 days; Urgent referral to hospital (for severe dehydration); Mother to continue rehydration by giving frequent sips of ORS during transport or use NG tube when possible in patients with poor drinking
